---
title: "Quest 5: Vectors - Collecting Data"
subtitle: "Store Many Values with Vec<T>"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
📦 QUEST 5 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/05-lists.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/05-lists.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
A vector is Rust's growable list type.

## 💡 Basic Vector Operations

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut scores = vec![10, 20, 30];
    scores.push(40);

    println!("All scores: {:?}", scores);
    println!("First score: {}", scores[0]);
}
```

## 👨‍💻 Example

```rust
fn main() {
    let tasks = vec!["read", "code", "rest"];

    for task in tasks {
        println!("Task: {}", task);
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Create a vector of 3 favorite foods, add one more with `push`, and print each item.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut foods = vec!["rice", "apples", "soup"];
    foods.push("bread");

    for food in foods {
        println!("{}", food);
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 5 Complete
You can create, grow, and iterate through vectors.
Next: [Quest 6: Functions](06-functions.qmd)
:::
